diagrams.title: Basic Logic Gates Symbols
diagrams.description: Standard symbols for seven basic logic gates: AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR, XOR, XNOR
elements: AND gate (flat-backed shield shape with pointed right side)
OR gate (curved back with pointed tip)
NOT gate (triangle with small circle at output)
XOR gate (OR with extra curved line)
diagrams.purpose: Learn standard IEEE/ANSI symbols for fundamental logic gates used in combinational circuits

diagrams.title: Combinational vs Sequential Circuit Block Diagram
diagrams.description: Comparison showing the fundamental difference in circuit architecture
elements: Combinational: Input → Logic Gates → Output (no feedback)
Sequential: Input + Current State → Logic Gates → Next State → Memory Element → Output (with feedback)
Clock signal shown in sequential circuit
Memory element (flip-flop) symbol in feedback path
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the structural difference: combinational has no memory, sequential has memory with feedback

diagrams.title: Truth Table Generation Process
diagrams.description: Systematic approach to creating truth tables for n input variables
elements: List all 2^n combinations in binary counting order (000, 001, 010...)
Calculate output for each combination based on Boolean expression
Verify completeness by checking all possibilities
diagrams.purpose: Understand method to systematically generate and verify truth tables for any combinational circuit

diagrams.title: De Morgan's Theorem Visual Representation
diagrams.description: Graphical proof and application of De Morgan's theorems using gate symbols
elements: Theorem 1: (A+B)' shown as OR gate followed by NOT equals AND of inverted inputs
Theorem 2: (A.B)' shown as AND gate followed by NOT equals OR of inverted inputs
Bubble matching technique for quick conversion
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the equivalence between compound gates and their complement forms for circuit simplification
